response: Unlike AST passes that transform before interpretation, interpreter optimizations leave the AST unchanged and short-circuit execution.

A macro body can call only macros declared before it in the flattened load order—i.e., from files it `use`s or earlier declarations in the same file—and this is enforced at expansion time via a visibility snapshot captured at declaration.
The current `breakpoint` statement is bare: it fires and dumps raw tape state, but with multiple breakpoints there is no way to tell which fired or where it is in the source.
The architecture targets <0.1ms total software overhead on any latency-critical path (input/audio/rendering) and must not exceed 1ms.
SPSC command queue overflow must be detectable so the scene can discard the affected measurement rather than silently producing invalid calibration data.
Per-thread SPSC lock-free queues: each logging thread writes to its own thread-local ring buffer with zero contention; the single backend thread polls all queues.
Runtime failures → clean shutdown; no hot recovery; user restarts. Acceptable for a calibration utility that is not a long-running service.
Restating content the target LLM already knows or can discover from existing sources (docs, code, READMEs) increases reasoning tokens 10–22% without improving outcomes.
Every directive in always-included content competes for the LLM's reasoning budget: extra instructions produce more tool calls, file traversal, and reasoning tokens, making tasks harder, not easier.
Grading = evaluating each assertion against the actual outputs and recording PASS/FAIL with specific evidence; evidence should quote or reference the output, not just state an opinion.
One important nuance: agents typically consult skills only for tasks requiring knowledge or capabilities beyond what they can handle alone.

When a separate agent writes tests, it receives only the spec — not function names/signatures, internal structure, or behavioral enumerations derived from reading the code; the parent agent must not compensate for spec gaps by front-loading implementation knowledge into the prompt.

Specs prescribe behavior, but predicting whether an LLM will follow a spec correctly is a false-belief task; exams verify spec effectiveness by running questions through actual LLM calls and observing output.
A correct answer must follow from an explicit, exceptionless rule; if a reasonable person could argue otherwise, the question fails as a test.
SKILL.md is the single context file agents receive when the worklog skill activates; it must be self-contained: an agent follows the methodology from SKILL.md alone, without reading individual specs.
Drift suppresses process compliance — governance checks/verification steps/archival procedures · steps the agent must remember to perform, but drift pushes out of active attention.

Injected context files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) add noise agents faithfully obey, empirically reducing success rates by ~3% while increasing cost >20% · agents follow unnecessary requirements (style guides, architecture overviews) even when they make the task harder.
The Knowledge Base stores reusable text you can reference from templates — character descriptions, world details, or reference material.
Important: skai-chat does not use tree-based history; e.g., retrying response generation mid-history modifies only one point, leaving later messages unchanged.

Subagent with detailed prompt engineering knowledge · offloads prompt-related reasoning from the main agent’s context window, letting it delegate prompt tasks without needing prompt engineering principles in its own context.
